data.title: Roku and Trade Desk shares rise after analyst highlights benefits of possible merger
data.description:
Guggenheim suggested that a hypothetical merger between The Trade Desk (NASDAQ:TTD) and Roku (NASDAQ:ROKU) could work to each other's strengths and ambitions

TTD and ROKU were up more than 5% in early open market trading on Monday.

The research firm said the relationship between the two can be bolstered via collaboration rather than competition.

They noted that TTD's Ventura OS, which is currently in the works, would face a long climb to achieve the market penetration necessary to impact the industry, and both companies see common threats from Amazon and Alphabet.

"With ROKU shares currently trading at 2x 2025E platform sales, we believe TTD could rapidly scale its OS ambitions via ROKU’s 85mm+ global streaming household footprint, while ROKU could quickly leverage its first-party viewer data and expanding CTV inventory to match with growing advertiser demand," the research firm said Monday.

TTD is up more than 82% while ROKU is down 22% so far this year. The benchmark S&P index is up more than 26%.

data.title: Tesla Holiday Update delivers Apple Watch options, Santa wraps for Cybertruck
data.description:
As part of its Holiday Update, Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) owners will now be able to perform a host of new tasks from their Apple (AAPL) smartwatch or through the Tesla app to find parking spots, incorporate weather delays into navigation, and perform holiday-themed light shows.

The Tesla (TSLA) app on the Apple (AAPL) Watch will allow drivers to use the device as a phone key, see how much battery charge is left, open the “frunk,” and turn on the vehicle’s climate control.

Cybertruck owners can now turn their system avatars into “Santa’s sleigh” or other custom wraps and enable passengers in the rear seats to play arcade games.

Model 3, Model Y and X owners can now program their headlamps into a themed holiday light show through the Holiday Update and amuse their passengers with a “bum detection” option to turn the passenger seat into a "fart on contact" whoopee cushion.

Along with the lighthearted new options, Tesla (TSLA) is also testing its latest full self-driving version, with plans to introduce it to the entire fleet by the end of the year.

FSD V13.2 promises to reduce the number of driver interventions, including a “parked to parked” function. This would enable the vehicle to move from a parked position to its final destination, in addition to performing three-point turns, and even find parking spots without user intervention. This option will be available to all vehicles except the Cybertruck.

Tesla (TSLA) is also making SiriusXM (SIRI) available for the Model 3, Y, and the Cybertruck.

data.title: U.S. puts restrictions on China's access to AI chips, tools
data.description:
Update: Adds China's response

The U.S. Department of Commerce has placed additional curbs on China’s access to vital tools and components for AI chips.

The new rules restrict sales of 27 types of high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and chipmaking equipment, including products made outside the country by American companies, and are in addition to existing curbs affecting advanced logic chips.

The department has also blacklisted an additional 140 Chinese entities that it said have been operating on behalf of the Chinese government. The names of these companies were not given in the initial statement.

The full list of new sanctions and company additions will be detailed later on Monday, a U.S. official told Bloomberg last week.

“This action is the culmination of the Biden-Harris Administration’s targeted approach, in concert with our allies and partners, to impair the PRC’s ability to indigenize the production of advanced technologies that pose a risk to our national security,” Secretary of Commerce Gina Raimondo said in a statement. “No Administration has been tougher in strategically addressing China’s military modernization through export controls.”

The controls unveiled Monday include exemptions for ally countries such as the Netherlands and Japan, according to a senior administration official, which are expected to impose similar restrictions. Neither government has so far issued such additional rules.

South Korea’s SK Hynix (OTCPK:HXSCF), a key Nvidia (NVDA) supplier, is a current leader in providing HBM chips, followed by Micron Technology (MU) and Samsung Electronics (OTCPK:SSNLF). The controls may also likely hurt U.S. chipmakers like Lam Research (LRCX), KLA Corp (KLAC) and Applied Materials (AMAT).

The Chinese Ministry of Commerce has called the U.S. restrictions “economic coercion,” and “seriously” threatening to the global supply chain. It said that the country will take measures to safeguard its interests, but did not disclose what these measures would be.

data.title: Sana gets FDA Fast Track designation for SC291 for lupus
data.description:
• Sana Biotechnology (NASDAQ:SANA) has received Fast Track designation for its cell therapy SC291 for the treatment of relapsed/refractory systemic lupus erythematosus, or SLE, which includes extrarenal lupus and lupus nephritis.
  • The company is currently enrolling patients in a clinical trial for SC291 in the treatment of B-cell mediated autoimmune diseases, including SLE. Initial data is expected next year, according to a statement.

data.title: Amazon partners with startup to pilot AI tech for data center carbon removal
data.description:
Amazon Web Services (NASDAQ:AMZN) has partnered with startup Orbital Materials to utilize its artificial intelligence platform to develop advanced materials capable of reducing the carbon footprint of data centers.

The proliferation of AI has led to a spike in data center electricity demand, which is in turn has created the risks of increased emissions by cloud providers such as AWS. Ironically, AWS and Orbital are attempting to use AI to minimize these carbon emissions.

A study by the International Energy Agency that data centers now account for 2% to 4% of total electricity consumption in the U.S., and account for as much as 10% in several states.

AWS plans to work with Orbital to synthesize and test new technologies and materials for data center carbon renewal, chip cooling and water utilization. Orbital is expected to pilot this technology by the end of 2025.

"Since establishing its lab in the first quarter of 2024, Orbital has achieved a 10x improvement in its material's performance through the use of its AI platform - an order of magnitude faster than traditional development and breaking new ground in carbon removal efficacy," according to AWS.

"Working with the market-leading AWS team will ensure that our suite of products in cooling, water utilization and carbon removal enables the next generation of data centers powering the AI revolution," said Orbital CEO and co-founder Jonathan Godwin.

Orbital will also offer its open-source AI model for simulating advanced materials through Amazon SageMaker JumpStart and AWS Marketplace.

"Together, we have the opportunity to set new benchmarks for carbon removal and efficiency across the industry," said Howard Gefen, General Manager of AWS Energy & Utilities.

AWS is holding its re:Invent 2024 conference this week. The event focuses on the global cloud-computing community. It takes place in Las Vegas.

Amazon shares edged up 2% during Monday morning trading.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow are mixed while Treasury yields climb
data.description:
Wall Street’s major market averages were mixed on Monday as traders and investors await a plethora of economic data releases over the week all while President-elect Donald Trump threatened to implement more trade tariffs.

As a result, the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) advanced the most, as it popped 0.9% so far. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was higher by 0.2% and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) handed back 0.3%.

From a sector-by-sector point of view, just three of the 11 S&P sectors are trading in positive territory with Communication Services leading the way. Going the other way, Utilities and Energy are the two worst sector performers so far.

U.S. Treasury yields observed a move higher to start the week as the shorter end more rate sensitive U.S. 2-Year Treasury yield (US2Y) ticked up by 5 basis points to 4.21% and the longer end U.S. 10-Year Treasury yield (US10Y) advanced by 5 basis points to 4.23%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

U.S. President-elect Donald Trump on Saturday threatened the BRICS member nations with 100% tariffs if they moved to create a new currency to replace the U.S. dollar. "As ever with Trump it seems to be a shot across the bows but likely wouldn't ultimately be great for the U.S. economy if implemented," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

As for stocks that are on the move ,shares of Super Micro Computer (SMCI) have surged 20.6%. Also, see what some of the bulls and bears on SMCI have to say.

Shares of Intel (INTC) also pushed up 3.6% after the company made the surprising announcement that Chief Executive Officer Pat Gelsinger is retiring and will be replaced by co-CEOs David Zinsner and Michelle Johnston Holthaus.

data.title: Ukraine is said to foresee surge in uncrewed vehicles, AI-powered war drones
data.description:
Ukraine next year will need tens of thousands of uncrewed robotic ground vehicles to carry ammunition and supplies to front-line soldiers and to evacuate wounded soldiers, Reuters reported Monday, citing a senior government minister.

The buggy-like vehicles would operate in areas near the front, which Russia is hammering with shells and exploding drones, and support Ukrainian soldiers, Deputy Prime Minister Mykhailo Fedorov said.

"This year we purchased several thousand ground platforms, and next year, I believe, we need tens of thousands," the minister said to Reuters. He had overseen procurement of drones throughout most of the war.

The ground vehicles already are being used along the front and in Russia's Kursk region, where Kyiv's troops carved out an enclave in an August incursion. Ukraine has several facilities to train soldiers on using the vehicles.

The heavy usage of drones and automated systems in the war between Russia and Ukraine has showcased how technology is changing warfare for ground troops. Unlike drones such as the Reaper that fly at high altitudes and launch air-to-ground missiles, newer drones operate near the ground and can act as disposable carriers of explosives.

data.title: Robinhood launches tax-focused feature for share sales
data.description:
Robinhood Markets (NASDAQ:HOOD) introduced a feature that allows customers to sell specific stock lots to help incur the lowest tax hit, the company said Monday.

"This gives customers the ability to make more informed decisions and manage their tax bill," the company said.

Tax Lots will allow customers to view the individual lots in their positions and select which ones to sell when executing a trade. For example, if the user bought 100 shares of a stock in two separate transactions at different prices, and the person want to sell 50 shares, the Tax Lots feature will let them choose which lots to sell.

The feature will start rolling out on Monday and will be available to all customers in coming weeks, Robinhood (NASDAQ:HOOD) said.

Robinhood (HOOD) stock jumped 3.0% in Monday morning trading.

data.title: Best ranking and performing Cybersecurity stocks: FSLY, NET, FTNT
data.description:
Mixed performances among cybersecurity stocks (NYSEARCA:HACK) today, after dropping about 5% on Wednesday after CrowdStrike’s (CRWD) earnings results.

Fastly (FSLY) and Cloudflare (NET) are performing the best today.

CrowdStrike’s (CRWD) third quarter non-GAAP EPS beat expectations by $0.12 to $0.93, with a revenue gain of 28.5% year-over-year of $1.01B, beating expectations by $26.97M. Here is the rest of its earnings results.

Wednesday’s post-earnings trading saw some investors sell shares after they recovered lost profits from July’s outage event.

CrowdStrike (CRWD) is down today 2.42%, but cybersecurity ETFs, such as the Amplify (HACK) and the First Trust Nasdaq Cybersecurity ETF (CIBR) are both up today 0.44% and 0.52% respectively. The global X Cybersecurity ETF (BUG), however, is down 0.42%.

data.title: B2Gold sticks to full-year production guidance for Mali mine despite strike
data.description:
B2Gold (NYSE:BTG) -4.1% in early trading Monday after saying it still expects production at the Fekola gold operation in Mali will come in toward the lower end of its annual production guidance of 420K-450K oz of gold in FY 2024 despite a seven-day strike that began November 29.

The company said it is continuing to operate the Fekola mill at full throughput capacity during the strike period, after some members of the Fekola workers union walked off the job.

B2Gold (BTG) said it believes the strike is primarily in response to action it took against a small number of Fekola employers previously engaged in illegal activities "detrimental to productivity at the Fekola mine, including 'go slow' actions by the mining employees and an illegal sit in at the Fekola management office in August."

B2Gold (BTG) said it produced 180,553 oz of gold in Q3, including 78,207 oz at Fekola, and expects to produce at least 800K gold oz from across its operations this year.

The company said it continues to make progress with Mali's government under the memorandum of agreement signed in September, including the issuance of the necessary permits to begin work at Fekola Regional located north of the Fekola mine.
